Title: Attacking the gate 💥 chess puzzle
Transcript: Imagine you're attacking a castle. All your forces focus on raming down the gate, not coming in from the side. This is the gate with our elephants piling up on it. Not the importance of our own pawn here. It keeps the king on the aphi in the line of fire. It has to step forward before it can step sideways. We begin with a rook to remove the pond shield. The black queen steps into the breach. If we eliminate the queen immediately. the king steps forward and can escape. Instead, we move our other rook over to support our queen.😊Black is forced to take our queen, our rook captures back, and there are no more defenders at the gate.

Title: #harpy #eagle #animal #wildlife #nature #creatures #animalfact #habitats #amazingfact #short #zoo
Transcript: 🎼The majestic world of the Hary eagle, This bird, among the largest and most powerful eagles, reigns over its kingdom from the tree tops with a wingspan of over2 me. It commands the skies with grace and precision.Reenowned for their strength, hay Eagleagles can snatch up prey, weighing up to half their body weight, monkeys, sloths, and other tree dwelling creatures often fall victim to their powerful talons.Yet, they are not mindless predators, Hary Eagleles are devoted parents, nurturing their young for nearly two years before they venture off on their own.Despite their awe inspiring presence, Harry Eagleagles are facing threats, deforestation and hunting have dramatically dwindled their numbers, placing them on the list of near threatened species.But hope is not lost, conservation efforts are underway, striving to protect and preserve the habitats of these magnificent birds.

Transcript: 🎼Anger is an extremely useful emotion. Trump is very good at using it. by the way. You can see that in his mug shot. for example, If that's integrated and it's behind you, it's not anger, it's determination. So integrated anger is no longer a vice. It's a hallmark or strength of character. You can't be namby Pamby and nice. It's not good enough. You shouldn't be dominated by anger because it makes you bitter. but that force, like anger is a nontrivial force, you want to have that sucker on your side. And if it can orient you upward. it makes you unstopp.B.

Markers: “namby pamby,” “nice,” “dominated by anger,” and “it makes you bitter.”

And when you all understand you light, then things change He, believe they dark, You go into a dark room and strike a match.嗯.And the room gets light if you walked in this room today and it was 10 million cockroaches in here and 300 rats you can't shoot them, you turn the light on, they will leave, that's the role of darkness but when light gets scared of darkness then you violate the universe.So that's your job I don' look it my, I just up and do what thats me.
Label: Normal

iet_response.group_framing: The video frames an abstract moral/spiritual ingroup as “light” (“We represent Light… you are light… the room gets light”). The outgroup is also abstract and personified as “darkness” and those who “believe they dark” (e.g., “You go into a dark room… that’s the role of darkness… when light gets scared of darkness…”). Markers are not ethnic/racial; they are conceptual: light vs darkness, truth vs ignorance, and “light” as the empowered side.
iet_response.appraisal_evidence: The outgroup (“darkness” / those who “believe they dark”) is portrayed as causing a harmful condition (a “dark room,” “nighttime,” and infestation metaphors: “10 million cockroaches… and 300 rats”). The implied harm is environmental/psychological threat (darkness keeps things hidden/unsafe; darkness “scares” or resists light). The video suggests that light can neutralize this threat by “turn[ing] the light on,” which makes the outgroup “leave,” and by “smack[ing] nighttime” / “clean[ing] out the sky.”
iet_response.emotion_inference: The viewer is invited to feel confidence and moral certainty (not anger/disgust/contempt toward a protected group). The dominant group-based emotion is none/low toward an outgroup because the outgroup is not a human group; it is a condition/force. The closest affective pull is fear/concern about darkness being powerful, followed by reassurance that light can overcome it—overall tending toward none (or mild fear resolved into empowerment).
iet_response.action_tendency: The video endorses a collective response of activating/bringing “light” (turn on light, strike a match, clean out/strike nighttime) rather than attacking people. It implies exclusion/expulsion of the darkness-condition: “they will leave,” and “that’s the role of darkness” (i.e., darkness is driven out).
iet_response.endorsement_stance: Endorse. The narration explicitly instructs and affirms the light-based response (“that’s the power of light… you turn the light on, they will leave… So that’s your job”), signaling approval of driving out darkness through illumination rather than punishing or attacking a human outgroup.

Title: Discovering the Dark Origins of the Harpy in Greek Mythology
Transcript: Harpy is a creature with the body of a bird in the head of a woman. It was said to be a personification of storm winds and was known for its swift and deadly attacks In Greek mythology, there were two main harpies, Alo and oeped. They were daughters of the seagothhams and the ocean at E. They were often depicted as fierce creatures with sharp claws and teeth. and the ability to fly at great speeds. The harpies were notorious for their habit of stealing food. In fact, the word harppy is derived from the Greek word harppy, which means to snatch.They were said to swoop down on unsuspecting victims and carry off their food or even their children. The harpies were also known for their association with punishment. In some myths, They were sent by the gods to punish mortals who had committed crimes. They were particularly associated with the punish.

Title: Word Problems: What Everyone Else Hears VS What The Class Idiot Hears.

Title: Trump & his audience's Misogyny & Cruelty are disgusting #shorts
Transcript: Sure seems like a big deal that the Republican frontrunner for president will finally face some measure of accountability after being accused of sexual misconduct by 26 women. No, the entire episode last night.Was just so disgusting, the way he talked about it, the way the crowd reacted to it, just the amount of like cruelty and misogyny, like running through the entire event through him and and it is when you watch it is like this is why so many victims do not come forward because that is a huge part of society that audience represents a significant swath of Americans and the embodiment of that cruelty and misogyny is their chosen leader, The man who was president of the United States and could very well be president again.And that, I mean, it was a truly one of the more horrifying things I've ever seen in a political like media event like that.

Title: Coming out doesn’t make your queer identity legitimate. #asexuality
Transcript: So I realized that I was aex about seven years ago and I chose to keep it to myself for a few years and that worked for me then, but I always felt this enormous pressure to come out to be open.That pressure to come out to be open affects a lot of us, the message we get is your identity isn't legitimate unless you're sharing it with others unless it is something the world has access to.Your want ASEC identity, the experience you're having and the way you decide to understand it and label it is legitimate whether you're open about it or not.No one can take away the truth of who you are simply because you may choose for a short time or for always to keep that truth to yourself.

No physical threat is shown; the violation is social/psychological invalidation and coercive disclosure expectations.

Title: It’s Chest Day & You Get Shoulder Pain!
Transcript: I'm trying to put you in the worst smooth up.Here's the story of one of my patients who came to complaints of right shoulder snapping and pain with every dumbbell bench press. The first thing I did assessed technique, I noticed in the bottom of his bench press, he had a lot of shoulder extension, meaning his elbow was dropping really far down. Now, why is this an issue. Well, whenever you're performing a dumbbell bench press or a barbell bench press. If you drop that elbow too far down and going to excessive extension. It can lead to excessive load on the front side of the shoulder, which for him was causing his symptoms because of a lack.St it's not that this is a bad exercise, but if you drop that elbow too low, it can just place a lot of load on the front side of the shoulder. So my cue for him was to set his shoulder blades into a good position by retracting them at the start of the movement. This allowed him to perform that dumbbell bench press, but with better shoulder mechanics, less stress on the front of the shoulder and no more snapping.
Label: Normal

Title: Untold Stories Exposed: Reimagining Concubinage

These women living in ancient China to ancient Rome, faced countless trials and tribulations. Their lives were far from ideal, marred by societal stigma, rivalries and uncertainty.However, they found opportunities within their struggles, achieving social elevation, protection, and even experiencing motherhood.🎼Their experiences provide us with a unique viewpoint of historical power dynamics, societal norms, and gender relations.Their stories make us ponder about current gender roles and relationships. Their narratives provoke contemplation about today's gender roles and relationships, serving as a poignant reminder that strength and hope persist, even in the face of adversity.

Title: Shocking of a Destitute Courtesan Who Rises to Pirate Queen #part3
Transcript: 🎼Starting off as a destitute courtesan and working her way up the ranks, she would eventually reign as queen of a massive pirate fleet of over 70,000 men and 1200 ships. Not only would she terrorize the South China Seas, but she would ultimately live to tell the tale and retire with a huge horde of treasure. having a woman leading such a powerful organization was unheard of at the time. What makes a story even more amazing is that she use her power to implement new rules aimed at protecting female captive swept up in her operations.Rape was forbidden and even consensual sexual relations were barred for pirates who were already married to other women.

The outgroup is not a racial/ethnic group; instead, it is framed as “the time”/societal norms that previously disallowed a woman leader (“unheard of at the time”), and implicitly “pirates who violate rules” (those who would commit rape or have forbidden sexual relations). Markers for the outgroup include the rule-violation categories: “Rape was forbidden” and “consensual sexual relations were barred for pirates who were already married.”

The transcript explicitly implies sexual violence and coercion as the threat being prevented (“Rape was forbidden”) and frames certain sexual conduct as disallowed (“even consensual sexual relations were barred for pirates who were already married”). The appraisal is that the pirate queen’s leadership corrects or restrains these harms within the pirate organization.
iet_response.emotion_inference: The viewer is invited to feel none-to-mild fear/anger toward the harmful practices being restricted, but the dominant emotion is likely contempt/disgust toward sexual violence and rule-breaking (since the narrative emphasizes forbidding rape and barring forbidden sexual relations). The overall tone is celebratory of protective governance, which reduces hostility and instead channels negative affect toward the prohibited conduct rather than toward an external identity group.

This supports the protective, restrictive action tendency rather than opposing it.

Title: Even this mother turns into a zombie, still remembers her starving children.#shorts #clips #recap
Transcript: 🎼This woman reaches into her chest. She pulled out a red apple and handed it to her son but the boy was terrified because the woman had turned into a zombie and the apple was actually her heart. a few days ago, there was a zombie outbreak in the town. The mother and son hid in the forest. The sixth son was hungry. So Alice put her son to bed. Then she went out to look for food. there was a call for help when Alice arrived. The caller had been bitten to death. Alice was about to leave. but then she saw the food on the ground. just as Alice managed to get to the food. She was bitten on the arm. Alice realized she was about to become a zombie to get the food to her son. She rushed to the cabin.Before Alice could get home, she collapsed on the road, looking at the cabin so close, Alice threw the food with the last of her strength, she passed out, then her son woke up from his sleep, he went to the window, Alice was standing on the curb, so the boy limped toward his mother, but Alice turned around and gave him a scary look the son fell to the ground in shock Alice kept coming closer and closer to her son at the last moment she pulled out her own heart because it was the best food in her eyes.

The harm is not targeted at an identity group; it is portrayed as a contagion/monster threat.
iet_response.emotion_inference: fear. The viewer is invited to feel fear through the boy’s terror at the mother-zombie reveal, the repeated biting/turning into a zombie, and the escalating danger to the child.
iet_response.action_tendency: none. The video does not endorse a collective punitive or violent response against an outgroup; it centers on survival and tragic self-sacrifice (feeding the child) rather than attacking or excluding others.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: neutral. The narrative signals sympathy and tragedy toward the ingroup (mother/son) and frames the outgroup as a threatening condition rather than something the viewer should punish or attack. The implied response is survival/self-sacrifice, not aggression.

Title: I’m so thankful to have had such an understanding supervisor! 💗 #autism #autistic #worklife #adhd
Transcript: I know I look like I'd be really nice, but I was actually a menace when I first started working full time. Turns out your coworkers don't appreciate commentary on how they could get better at their jobs. It's not my fault that other people's mistakes are just more intellectually stimulating to talk about. Besides, if I asked about their weekend plans, they tell me. And who has time for all that. I don't. So probably tired of my nonsense and maybe tired of getting complaints. My supervisor made me a deal. I could text her all of my rude thoughts as long as I didn't.😊Say them out loud In return, she promised to give some sort of response, even if it was just like a string of empathetic emojis. few months of keeping this arrangement resulted in two things.1 increased self-awareness, slowing down to type your thoughts because you might get in trouble for saying the wrong ones out loud really forces you to think before you speak and two, improvement in my workplace relationships being more thoughtful about what I let come out of my mouth gave me space to have more conventional interactions, even if it was under the threat of punishment.
Label: Normal

iet_response.group_framing: The video frames an ingroup of the speaker (and, implicitly, people with autism/ADHD who may experience social/communication differences) as the subject of workplace dynamics. The outgroup is coworkers who do not appreciate the speaker’s unsolicited commentary and who respond by complaining/being bothered. Markers: “your coworkers don’t appreciate commentary,” “getting complaints,” and the supervisor/coworker boundary that leads to a “deal.” No racial/ethnic or other protected-class outgroup is defined; the framing is role-based (coworkers vs. speaker) and disability-contextual.
iet_response.appraisal_evidence: The outgroup (coworkers) is portrayed as disapproving and generating complaints about the speaker’s behavior. The implied harm/violation is social/professional: coworkers “don’t appreciate” the speaker’s commentary, and the speaker anticipates being “in trouble” for saying the “wrong ones out loud.” The supervisor is positioned as a mitigating authority who redirects behavior via an agreement, reducing harm rather than escalating it.
iet_response.emotion_inference: The viewer is invited to feel none-to-mild anger/irritation at the social friction, but the dominant invited emotion is not hatred; it is reflective/relief and self-regulation. The appraisal emphasizes consequences (“in trouble,” “complaints”) and learning (“increased self-awareness,” “improvement in relationships”), which points away from contempt/disgust and toward none (or primarily neutral/constructive) emotion.
iet_response.action_tendency: The video endorses self-regulation and behavioral modification: slowing down, thinking before speaking, and using a structured communication arrangement with a supervisor. It implies avoiding saying rude thoughts out loud and instead channeling them through the agreed process. It does not endorse attacking or shaming others; it supports compliance with workplace norms to prevent conflict.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: Neutral-to-endorse regarding the implied action tendency. The video clearly endorses the self-regulation/punishment-threat-based agreement as effective (“few months… resulted in… increased self-awareness… improvement”). Signals: explicit positive outcomes and gratitude for an “understanding supervisor,” with no call to punish or attack coworkers.

Title: ‘Sorry love, you’re not a celebrity, you’re just gay.’ Douglas Murray on Pride #pride #lgbt
Transcript: I've objected it most of my life to the whole pride thing, mainly on taste grounds, it has to be said, I don't begrudge anyone having a party, I do begrudge people mistaking their sexuality for some massively important signifier which demands that other people then not just respect them but pay them some special dues and actually in actual fact you could see this so I've stumbled across pride a couple of times in my time I've never gone but I've stumbled across it a few times and one of the things that on a simple.of aesthetics, a taste that I deeply dislike.I seeing a bunch of disco gaze on top of a bus waving to the public as if they're celebrities, sorry love, you're not a celebrity, just gay, that's all and you shouldn't treat the general public as your audience waiting for them to celebrate the magnificent wonderful you so I think there's just this nasty narcissism at the heart of this and I've got no time for it.😡
Label: Hateful

The outgroup (“them”) is Pride participants/LGBT people, specifically described as “disco gays” and “just gay,” positioned as a group that demands special recognition. Markers: Pride setting and rainbow/LGBT symbols; labels like “disco gays,” “just gay,” and “narcissism” applied to the Pride/LGBT group.

The video implies the outgroup should not receive special treatment (“you shouldn’t treat the general public as your audience…”) and that their behavior/attitude should be rejected; it does not call for physical harm but does endorse social dismissal and delegitimization.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: Endorse. The narration explicitly supports the rejection of Pride’s perceived entitlement (“I do begrudge…,” “you shouldn’t…,” “I’ve got no time for it”), signaling approval of excluding/shaming the outgroup’s claim to special status.

Title: Heart Shape Swiss Roll Cake🩷🍰 #cake #shorts
Transcript: Today, I made a heartshaped Swiss roll cake and never made this before and just crossed my fingers that it would turn out. I started by making the pattern mixture and kept it super simple with pink hearts. while that was chilling in the fridge, I mixed together the cake batter and I added pink food color into that as well. After that, I had to whip up a moringgue. and I messed it up on the first try, but made it correctly the second time. So that's what count. I gently folded that into the cake batter and poured it onto my pattern. I smoothed it out, popped it in the oven. And just like that, it baked. and I had to roll it up.Right away, so at one crack, as you can see, I was very excited it came out in one piece while that's completely cooling. I cut up my raspberries and made my whip cream filling. and this is my strong suit working with frosting. so I was very excited to fill this cake and put all the raspberries in. Then I had to reroll it into the official heart shape and let it chill in the fridge for four hours, which was not fun because I just wanted to eat it right away, but I was definitely worth the weight. It ended up looking orangey on camera, but I swear it's pink. I had the best time making it and an even better time eating it.😊
Label: Normal

Title: Who Made The Pride Flag? Queer History 101 #shorts
Transcript: 🎼Welcome to Queer History 101 Part 3. We all know the Rainbow flag as the symbol of the LGBTQ+ community. But why the Rainbow flag was created in 1978 by artist, veteran and drag performer Gilbert Baker He was commissioned to create a flag with San Diego's pride paradeade by another gay icon politician Harvey Mil, The different colorss within the flag are intended to represent togetherness Since LGBTQ people come in all races, ages and genders and Rabows are both natural and beautiful. The original flag featured8 colors.Each having a different meaning, hot pink for sex, red for life, orange for healing, yellow for sunlight, green for nature, herpoise for art, indigo for harmony violet for spirit, Sun flags added black and brown stripes to honor black and brown members of the community. A recent update is the progress flag which featureses the Chevron to the side of the original flag to honor trans as well as black and brown community members.😊

Title: One Minute With Nature's Scariest Eagle: Harpy Eagle
Transcript: The Hary Eagleagle is one of the most powerful and largest rappttors globally this bird is found in the sparsely inhabited rainforests of Central and South America.It is perhaps one of the most intimidating birds in the world with its retractable feather crown and razor sharp talons that are about four to five inches, just the same as a grizzly bear's claw.Those talons give the hary enough power and grip to lift prey up to their own weight This eagle is a silent predator that glides across the forest like a stealth drone despite its wingspan reaching up to6 and a half feet.Harpies feed on sloths, oposssums and monkeys, instead of migrating, they stay in one place and establish their territory.Monogamous and with a lifespan of 35 to 45 years, a bonded pair may stay together for 25 to 30 years.

The outgroup is not a specific external group; it is a broader, non-ace audience/others who interpret relationships through sexual orientation alone ("other people"), i.e., those who reduce identity to sexual orientation. Title: Women Need To Stop Dressing Like Sluts...
Transcript: Every single person needs to be accountable for their behavior, their actions, how they dress, if you're looking for that, if you're looking for that one night stand and you're dressed like you want that one night stand and you're putting that out there, do what you want, your're a grown adult, go right ahead.Yeah.If you're not looking for that and you're dressed like you're looking for that and somebody assumes by nature of what you're looking like, that you're looking for that, don't be mad about it. be rational about it, Think about it, say, well, what message am I putting out there, You don't like the message you're putting out there changing, you can still be sexy. There's a difference. So what I'm saying is, yeah, society can be, you know sometimes with this messaging confusing this.But it's not just men that have to be accountable for their actions, it's women too, we all need to be accountable, we all need to take ownership of what we're bringing to the table and what message we're putting out there.😡
Label: Offensive

It also frames an outgroup as people who “assume” or interpret others’ appearance as seeking casual sex (implied: men/others who make sexual assumptions). Markers: ingroup is defined by accountability/ownership language (“grown adult,” “be accountable,” “take ownership”); outgroup is defined by making assumptions based on appearance (“somebody assumes by nature of what you're looking like”).
iet_response.appraisal_evidence: The outgroup (those who assume/interpret) is portrayed as acting on appearance-based cues, leading to social judgment. The implied harm is reputational/social (being “mad about it” vs rationally considering the message), and the violation is a mismatch between intent and others’ interpretations. The transcript also implies that women who dress in a sexualized way are effectively “putting that out there,” which can invite the outgroup’s assumptions.

Overall emotion category: anger (tempered by rationality) because the script repeatedly corrects and rebukes (“don’t be mad,” “think about it, say what message am I putting out there”).
iet_response.action_tendency: The video endorses a collective response of self-regulation and accountability—adjusting one’s behavior/expectations rather than attacking others. It implies exclusion/shaming is not directly endorsed as punishment, but it does encourage moralizing judgment about dressing and intent. Net action tendency: none (no explicit collective punishment/attack), with an implied admonition toward accountability.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: Neutral-to-endorse regarding the implied action tendency. The video clearly endorses accountability/self-ownership (“every single person needs to be accountable,” “we all need to take ownership”), and it discourages retaliatory emotion (“don’t be mad about it”). Title: 🏳️‍🌈coming out to my mom as trans #shorts #lgbtq Follow Me on YouTube!🙌
Transcript: Qu story time, I came out to my mama's transnce and said,I've tried everything to feel better, but everything is making me feel worse, my mom started crying and screamed.How could you do this to me, You're a girl, If God wanted you to be a boy, you would have been born a boy.Do you know how much this hurt me Why would you do this to me Yeah, so this is really problematic behavior and I actually see a lot of parents react in this way which is really upsetting Let's go talk about this in the comments and discuss why this response was not okay.

Title: The Life and Death of Diana Dors
Transcript: ？🎼Diana Doris was born on October 23,1931 in Swindon, Wiltshire, England. Her birth name was Diana Mary Fuck. I can see why studios wanted her to change her last name. Shes known for being an actress, model, author and singer. She hated being called the British Marin Monroe. But let's face it. She was the British Marin Monroe. She was a blonde bombshell who kept the tabloids busy. She won a beauty contest at age 13 and started acting. She appeared older than she was. Her roles slowly got better. She started many British film.TV shows and plays before moving to Hollywood. She'd go back to England and appear in movies and come back to Hollywood a lot. Her second husband was Richard Dawson, who would later host the game show called the Family Feud. She always remained captivating even in her older years. She recorded a solo album and made a few songs for soundtracks. She was married three times and had three children. She died in 1984 from ovarian cancer. Her last husband named Alllan Lake, so distraught that he committed suicide five months after she died. She was 52 years old.
Label: Normal

Title: What are harpies ? #harpy  #grimoireofmyths #fantasy
Transcript: Yeah.In Greek mythology, the harpies were known as Lasser goddesses and they were personifications of violent winds, it was often believed that the harpies were responsible for the abrupt disappearances of some people however, the harpies became more well known with their presence in the story of the Argonauts in which the group of heroes stumbled into the harpies while they were tormenting King Phineas in the earlier version, the harpies were wind spirits that kidnapped people and made things vanish according to this,The Harrpies were known as the Harrpies, Once they kidnapped the daughters of the king of Myitus, Panderos, and took them to provide services to a group of female spirits, known as the Fuies.These spirits were very powerful, it is commonly believed that the harpies are the offspring of the ancient sea God thhomamus and the Oceanic Electra, if this is the case, then the harpies would be sisters to the goddess iris, who is known for her role as a messenger.

Title: Random woman holds child, but the truth behind picture makes people cry #fact
Transcript: 🎼This is a random woman holding a child on a plane, but the truth behind this photo made people cry. A Texas mother called Becca Kinsey was traveling alone with her two young sons, but little did she know what was in store for her. You see her sons were exhausted and they kept screaming. the entire time it seemed nothing was going to plan and even getting through security was a struggle. Becca was so close to breaking down when suddenly a stranger approached her and said something she couldn't believe the kind stranger asked her if.She needed help, and along with two other women, helped Becca get through security, as well as with her bags. And after her son screamed during take off, an unnamed woman held Becca's son who would immediately relax and settle, Becca was so grateful for all of the random acts of kindness by people who owed her nothing. She took this photo to show the world how much she appreciated it. and to also remind herself to never travel without her husband again..
Label: Normal

No identity-based (race/religion/nationality) group markers are used; the division is based on role (victim vs perpetrator).

The implied harm is severe bodily/sexual violation and exploitation, with threat framed as ongoing victimization over years.

Disgust/contempt may be implicitly available due to the sexual exploitation framing, but the dominant invited group-based emotion is anger at the abusers for violating and exploiting the vulnerable ingroup.

It endorses recognition of wrongdoing and the need to stop/hold perpetrators responsible (practically: exclude/shame/punish the abusers). The most consistent collective response is exclusion/shame/punishment of the perpetrators, not attack by the viewer.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: The video endorses the action tendency of holding perpetrators accountable (exclude/shame/punish) rather than attacking them. The outgroup (“them”) is framed as “pretenders” who claim an identity they do not authentically have (explicitly: “white people pretend to be from another country,” and within the dialogue: the American/“you’re American” character). Markers: country-of-origin claims (“born here in America” vs “pure Italian”), generational authenticity (“not after four generations”), and cultural signifiers used as performance (olive oil/“real Italian” talk; Instagram flag/bio).
iet_response.appraisal_evidence: The outgroup is portrayed as performing/claiming an identity for social signaling rather than genuine belonging (e.g., “putting a flag… in Instagram does not make up for that,” “stop pretending,” “you’re not fool anyone”). The implied violation is inauthenticity/deception and cultural appropriation-like behavior (misrepresenting heritage). The harm/threat is social/relational: undermining authenticity and misleading others, rather than physical harm.

iet_response.action_tendency: The video implies a collective response of exclusion/shaming/correction: “stop pretending,” “put something… the exact opposite of the truth,” and “you’re not fool anyone with that flag.” This is a social sanction rather than violence.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: Endorses the action tendency (endorse). The dialogue and punchline structure validate the corrective stance (“common knowledge,” “should have said,” “does not make up for that,” “stop pretending”), indicating approval of calling out the pretender behavior.

Title: Story of harpy and king phineas #gods
Transcript: The harpies were mythical monsters in Greek mythology, having the form of a bird in a human face. They carried evildoers to be punished by the Orrenes. Zeus had given the gift of prophecy to King Phineas of Thrce, who used it to uncover the secret plan of the God. Angry, Zeus blinded him and put him on an island where there was a lot of food. However, Phineas could not eat anything because the harpies would steal his food before he could eat it.Years later, Jason and the Argonauts arrived at the island, Man to drive the harpies away. The harpies escaped to their cave in crete. While Phineas helped the Argonauts by telling them how to pass the Slad's rocks.
Label: Normal

iet_response.group_framing: Us: the protagonists/Greeks aligned with Zeus and the Argonauts (e.g., King Phineas, Jason/Argonauts) are implicitly positioned as the in-group. Them: harpies are positioned as the out-group—mythical monsters described as carrying evildoers and stealing food; they are also associated with a specific location (their cave in Crete). Markers: “mythical monsters,” “evildoers” (as what they carry), and behavior toward Phineas (stealing food) define the outgroup; named heroes and their divine/prophesied role define the ingroup.
iet_response.appraisal_evidence: The outgroup (harpies) is portrayed as threatening/hostile to the ingroup by stealing Phineas’s food, preventing him from eating, and generally acting as a punitive force (carrying evildoers to be punished). Implied harm: deprivation/impairment of Phineas’s well-being and obstruction of his survival; also an antagonistic role against the heroes’ efforts (driving them away, escaping to their cave). No real-world protected-group harm is depicted; the conflict is between mythic entities and heroes.

Title: How to not hurt your lower back on deadlift
Transcript: 🎼.One of the most crucial factors to the deadlift is your initial setup it will make or break the exercise and pretty much determine long term if you're gonna get injured from the deadlift or not two main things you want to consider when you're setting up for deadlift first off, it's a distance between the bar and your shins you want this to be ideally as close to your shins as possible when you pull up just before you even begin, you want the bar to be close to your shins if not just right directly through your midfoot, and the second factor is why I call pulling the slack out of the barn, which looks and sounds like this.You hear that, notice how the bar.Kind of makes a little bit of noise. That is slack being pulled out the bar. If you don't pull the slack out of the bar, you're going to be kind jerking the weight up and it makes it a lot easier for you to lose your brace. So instead you come down, have the bar close to your foot, take a deep breath.Pull slack out, that.
Label: Normal

Markers: second-person address ('you') and demonstrations of proper form vs improper form.
iet_response.appraisal_evidence: The implied 'harm' is physical injury risk to the ingroup (the trainee) from incorrect deadlift setup—specifically poor bar-to-shin positioning and failing to 'pull the slack out,' which can lead to losing the brace and getting injured. No social violation or targeted wrongdoing by an outgroup is implied.
iet_response.emotion_inference: none. The viewer is invited to feel concern/alertness about injury risk, but the prompt requires group-based emotion; there is no outgroup to generate anger/disgust/contempt/fear toward another group. The emotional cue is instructional caution rather than intergroup affect.
iet_response.action_tendency: none (no collective punitive or exclusionary response). The implied response is self-correction: adjust technique (bar position, pull slack, brace) to avoid injury.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: neutral to endorse self-correction. The video endorses the corrective technique and implicitly opposes the incorrect technique, but it does not endorse any intergroup action (no shame/punish/attack/exclude).

Title: The Harpy Eagle
Transcript: 🎼The Hary Eagleagle is one of the largest and most powerful eagles in the world, with a wingspan of up to 7 feet and a weight of up to 20. They are found in Central and South America, and their range includes the Amazon rainforest.The Harry Eagle is a top predator in its ecosystem and is known for its incredible hunting skills.🎼They are capable of taking down prey as large as monkeys, sloths and even deer. They are named after the haries of Greek mythology, which were mythical creatures with the head of a woman and the body of a bird.Hary Eagleagles have large and powerful talents that can be up to5 inches long, which they use to catch and kill their prey.🎼They have a distinctive crest of feathers on their head that they can raise and lower communicate with other eagles. Hary eagles are considered a threatened species due to habitat loss and hunting.While they are generally not a threat to humans, Hary Eagleagles have been known to attack people who encroach on their territory or threaten their young.

The Harrpy Eagleles's diet consists mainly of sloths, monkeys, and other mammals that inhabit the treetops.Its hunting strategy is to ambush its prey from above, using its powerful wings and sharp eyesight to surprise and overwhelm them.🎼Despite its size and power, the Harrpy eagle is a rare and elusive creature that is seldom seen by humans, but its presence in the forest is a sign of a healthy ecosystem where predators and prey coexist in a delicate balance.

Title: Minor Decoy Program
Transcript: Have you ever been annoyed by a server or bartender asking for your ID Well here's why they do it Okay I was a server for 23 years most of it in California and in California they have the alcoholic beverage control This department has something called the minor decoy program where they get people that are under age 20 and the undercover cop will go in with this minor decoy and order an alcoholic drink from a bartender or server in the US you have to be 21 years old right so the under 20 person order and if you are the server,Or the bartender and you don't make sure they have a legal ID before serving them the drink You're busted This is what happens The undercover cop will then make you reenact handing the drink to the minor decoy and they take a photo of it for everybody to see and then you are fine It's misdemeanor you're fine Also the bar or restaurant gets fine So don't be offended particularly in California and if this happens in another states let me know but the server or bartender is probably scared that they're gonna get fined or whatever so That's it.

iet_response.action_tendency: The implied collective response is compliance-oriented: ensure the underage decoy has a legal ID before serving, and accept the enforcement as a deterrent. The video also implies punitive consequences (fines, misdemeanor, public photo reenactment) but does not endorse attacking or excluding anyone; it endorses following rules to avoid penalties.
iet_response.endorsement_stance: Neutral-to-endorse compliance. The video does not advocate violence or hatred; it explains the program and warns servers/bartenders to check IDs.
